core_problem: Our average 2026 bid ($161,677) is 2.1x our historical average won contract ($76,022). GC feedback confirms: Parkway said Hyatt bids were DOUBLE other bidders. Williams Company said 7% higher on Target Pineville. 20 estimating bids, zero wins. This is a pricing problem.

gc_feedback.feedback: Our proposal was TWICE other proposals
Told us our bid was double another company
Told us our bid was double another company
GC went different direction with owner

root_causes.detail: If using $45-55/hr burdened but competitors use $28-35/hr (NC open-shop), 40-60% higher on largest cost component.
If estimating 100-120 SF/hr but crews actually achieve 200-300 SF/hr (spray) or 150-200 SF/hr (roll), doubling labor hours.
30-40% overhead + 20-30% profit on already-inflated labor = 2x market.
Competitors spray open walls at 400-600 SF/hr. Estimating brush-and-roll rates for spray-eligible surfaces = 2-3x too high.
Bidding multi-trade work with painting-company overhead when competitors specialize and price each trade leaner.

won_contract_insights: 75% of won contracts are under $100K. Sweet spot is $25K-$100K projects.
35 of 58 won projects (60%) came through Parkway — but at OLDER pricing. Relationship carried us, not price.
Glenn Work projects averaged $44K — small, relationship-based jobs where price wasn't heavily compared.
Most profitable facility types: Cinema ($132K avg), Religious ($149K avg), Retail ($193K avg) — these are our strengths.
2021 was cheapest year ($27K avg). 2025 jumped to $108K avg — scope grew but so did price risk.
Haven't won a single new-GC bid in 2026. Every recent win came through existing relationships.

immediate: Audit actual burdened labor rate — if over $35/hr, that's #1 problem. NC market is $25-32/hr.
Re-estimate one lost bid (Hyatt Select Charlotte) using corrected production rates. Should see 30-50% reduction.
Get crew timing data from Sergei — SF painted per hour by task. Real data beats rate tables.
Switch to spray-first estimating — default spray rates for open walls/ceilings, brush-and-roll only for cut-in and occupied.
short_term_30_days: Build bid calculator (Carol IS this calculator now).
Separate paint vs. other trades pricing — don't apply painting overhead to tile/FRP/partition work.
Track actual vs. estimated hours on every job — feedback loop to calibrate rates.
Re-bid 2-3 upcoming projects with new rates — compare old vs. new. If delta is 30-50%, we found the problem.
long_term_90_days: Target 20-25% hit rate. Industry avg is 15-25%. Currently 0% on new bids.
Specialize bidding in Cinema, Religious, Senior Living, Retail — our strongest facility types.
Build GC-specific pricing — Parkway has different expectations than LF Jennings.
Invest in airless sprayer system ($2-5K) — pays for itself in 1-2 jobs.
Subcontract non-paint trades — markup sub work at 10-15%, not 25-35%.

pricing_rules_for_carol.spray_first_explanation: Always default to spray rates for open walls and ceilings. Only use brush-and-roll for cut-in, occupied spaces, and detailed trim work.
